shortAnswer: INR measures blood clotting time and is mainly used to monitor anticoagulants (e.g. warfarin). Values above 1.2 may indicate a prolonged clotting time, increasing bleeding risk. Discuss any changes with your doctor.
metaDescription: INR elevated? Normal ranges, meaning in warfarin therapy, and coagulation disorders: explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Is my INR within the therapeutic range for my anticoagulation therapy?
Do I need to adjust my warfarin dose?
Can certain foods affect my INR?
pageTitle: INR value: International Normalized Ratio explained | Medyra
